skill.name: nano-banana
skill.description: Generate and edit images with Google Gemini native image models through the inference.sh CLI.
skill.summary: Create Gemini image outputs from text prompts, input images, and configurable generation settings.
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: AI review confirms that the curl-to-shell install command is a real supply-chain and remote code execution risk if followed. Most external command and network findings are expected for an inference.sh CLI skill, and no evidence of malware, credential theft, prompt injection, or hidden exfiltration was found in SKILL.md.

high_findings.title: Remote Install Script Piped to Shell
high_findings.description: The quick start tells users to fetch a remote installer and pipe it directly into the shell. This is a true positive because a changed or compromised installer could execute arbitrary local commands before the user can inspect it.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 16 contains a direct remote installer pipeline into sh. The surrounding text says checksum verification is available, but the quick start still asks users to execute remote code immediately.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution Is Core Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill is built around running infsh and related setup commands. These examples appear legitimate for the stated purpose, but they execute local commands and depend on a third-party CLI.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and numerous, but they match the advertised CLI-based image workflow. I did not find evidence that arguments are dynamically built from untrusted input.

medium_findings.title: Network-Dependent Image Generation
medium_findings.description: The skill sends prompts, image URLs, and optional search-grounded requests through remote services. This is expected for Gemini image generation, but users should treat submitted prompts and input images as data shared with external services.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file clearly documents remote service use and image URL inputs. The risk is privacy and third-party processing, not hidden exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts do not show weak algorithms in use. The cited content is model naming, example prompt text, an input options table, and a SHA-256 checksum note; SHA-256 is appropriate for checksum verification.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no MD5, SHA-1, DES, or similar weak algorithm usage at the flagged locations. The only checksum reference is SHA-256.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Expected Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL alerts point to the inference.sh service, an image asset, example image input, and documentation pages. These URLs are expected for this CLI integration, though they confirm the network risk factor.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation or example links and align with the stated service. I found no evidence that they are used for covert exfiltration.

dangerous_patterns.title: curl Pipe to Shell Install Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The pattern fetches installer content from a remote host and executes it in the local shell. This pattern should be replaced with a manual download and checksum verification workflow before publication.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is present on a single line and directly pipes remote content to sh. This is a well-known dangerous installation pattern even when the provider is legitimate.

content.value_statement: Image teams need a repeatable way to generate and edit Gemini image outputs from prompts. This skill documents inference.sh CLI commands for text-to-image, image editing, and model selection.
actual_capabilities: Runs Gemini native image generation through inference.sh CLI examples.
Shows text-to-image prompts for Gemini 3 Pro Image and Gemini 2.5 Flash Image.
Documents image editing with input image URLs.
Supports multiple image outputs through the num_images option.
Documents aspect ratio, resolution, output format, and Google Search grounding options.
Shows commands to list Gemini image apps and generate sample input files.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI and account authentication.
Sends prompts and input images to remote services.
High-resolution 4K output is documented for Gemini 3 Pro only.
The skill provides command guidance, not local image generation.

use_cases.title: Concept Art Exploration
use_cases.description: Create several prompt-driven image directions for campaigns, products, or scenes.

use_cases.title: Marketing Asset Drafts
use_cases.description: Generate multiple image options with controlled aspect ratios and output counts.

use_cases.title: Image Variation Workflows
use_cases.description: Edit an existing input image by describing additions, changes, or stylistic adjustments.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Simple Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use nano-banana to create one photorealistic image of a clean product desk setup in a bright studio.

prompt_templates.title: Create Multiple Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Use nano-banana to generate four square logo concept images for a modern coffee brand.

prompt_templates.title: Edit an Input Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use nano-banana to edit this image URL by adding a rainbow in the sky while keeping the landscape realistic.

prompt_templates.title: Configure Advanced Output
prompt_templates.prompt: Use nano-banana with Gemini 3 Pro Image to create a 16:9 4K cinematic mountain scene with northern lights.

output_examples.input: Create a futuristic city at sunset with flying cars.
output_examples.output: The skill runs a Gemini image command through inference.sh and returns generated cityscape image results.

output_examples.input: Generate four minimalist coffee shop logo concepts.
output_examples.output: The skill requests multiple image outputs and returns a set of logo concept images for review.

output_examples.input: Edit a landscape image by adding a rainbow.
output_examples.output: The skill submits the input image URL with an edit prompt and returns a modified landscape image.

best_practices: Review any installer before running it locally.
Avoid submitting confidential images or sensitive prompt content.
Specify model, aspect ratio, resolution, and output count before generation.
anti_patterns: Do not run remote install scripts without verification.
Do not submit private customer images unless approved for external processing.
Do not expect deterministic results from repeated image generation prompts.

faq.question: What models does this skill use?
faq.answer: It documents Gemini 3 Pro Image and Gemini 2.5 Flash Image through inference.sh.

faq.question: Can it edit existing images?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports image editing by passing input image URLs with an edit prompt.

faq.question: Does it generate images locally?
faq.answer: No. It uses the inference.sh CLI to call remote Gemini image services.

faq.question: Can it create multiple images?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented num_images option requests several outputs from one prompt.

faq.question: Is Google Search grounding supported?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill shows an enable_google_search option for current-information prompts.

faq.question: What should users verify before use?
faq.answer: Users should verify CLI installation sources, authentication requirements, and external data handling rules.
